name: TDD-SUBAGENT-BACKLOG-19
title: Remove STEP 1 (SPEC-READ) and STEP 2 (HUMAN-APPROVAL) from tdd-subagent prompt — both are dispatcher's responsibility
description:
The dispatch-tdd-subagent.py prompt template includes STEP 1 SPEC-READ and STEP 2 HUMAN-APPROVAL as the first two of nine steps. Both are structurally wrong for the subagent and should live only in the dispatcher.

ARCHITECTURAL CONTRADICTION:
rabbit-feature/skills/rabbit-feature-touch/SKILL.md line 84-86 explicitly states the HUMAN-APPROVAL gate 'lives here, in the main session, because subagents run to completion and cannot pause for user input mid-execution.' Yet dispatch-tdd-subagent.py lines 344-357 still emit a STEP 2 — HUMAN-APPROVAL instruction in the subagent prompt telling it to 'present this summary to the user and wait for explicit approval.' Subagents have no user-interaction tools (only Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Glob, Grep) — they cannot wait for user input. When --human-approval-gate false is passed (the common case after dispatcher gate has run) the step renders as 'Skipped.' When true is passed it is a soft gate that does not enforce anything.

STEP 1 SPEC-READ is similarly redundant. The dispatcher Step 3 (Spec Authoring) invokes rabbit-feature-spec subagent which authors the spec, then the dispatcher commits it. By the time the tdd-subagent is dispatched the spec is already committed at HEAD. The subagent re-running git diff HEAD~1 -- docs/spec/ is preparation/context-loading, not a real step — the dispatcher already understands the spec delta. The spec content is also already interpolated into the subagent prompt at line 383 ({spec_content}), so the subagent does not even need to read the file to know what changed.

PROPOSED CLEANUP:
- Drop STEP 1 SPEC-READ from the prompt entirely; the {spec_content} interpolation already gives the subagent everything it needs.
- Drop STEP 2 HUMAN-APPROVAL from the prompt entirely; the dispatcher Step 4 owns this gate.
- Renumber remaining steps: LOCK → TEST-WRITE → TEST-RED → IMPLEMENT → CODE-REVIEW → TEST-GREEN → UNLOCK (7 steps).
- Remove --human-approval-gate argument from dispatch-tdd-subagent.py since the subagent no longer runs the gate.
- Update tdd-subagent/docs/spec/spec.md to retire any invariants that reference the dropped steps (CHANGELOG tombstone per the renumber-vs-gaps rule in the housekeeping protocol).
- Update tdd-subagent/agents/tdd-subagent.md and the agent description to say '7 named steps' instead of '9.'
- Update rabbit-feature-touch SKILL.md and tdd-subagent agent description strings that mention '9-step TDD cycle' to '7-step TDD cycle.'
- Cross-feature: rabbit-feature/docs/spec/contract.md invokes.scripts signature for dispatch-tdd-subagent.py likely drops --human-approval-gate.

IMPACT: Cosmetic-but-real friction removal. Cleaner subagent prompt, no dead-code soft gate, single owner for human approval (the dispatcher). Filed for evaluation; the consolidation could happen as a single small wave using rabbit-feature-touch against tdd-subagent.
